description: Two-loop closure inputs of the HBR identity. The T-parity argument that fixes theta_em = pi at the saddle level (Section: T-parity assignment of the defect field) plus the dilute-instanton-gas robustness bound on |Delta cos(theta_em)| are bundled here as the structural T-parity source axiom of the construction. Together with the four independent g-path determinations (data/g_path_*.json) and the bundled bounce-action eta_S, the data here close v_EW^canonical = 246.2186 GeV at residual Delta v / v = -5.86e-6 against PDG.

T_parity_source_axiom.statement: The defect field carries T-eigenparity eta_T(Xi) = -1 at the saddle of the bounce action, so the unique negative mode is T-odd.
structural_layers: Source axiom on the defect field's T-eigenparity at the bounce saddle.
Coleman saddle theorem fixing the unique negative-mode T-parity sign.
Defect-FT identification linking Xi to the bounce-saddle field whose T-eigenparity is the source axiom.

dilute_instanton_gas_robustness.interpretation: Non-perturbative dilute-instanton-gas corrections to cos(theta_em) are suppressed by exp(-S_bounce); the bound is 14 orders of magnitude below the closed two-loop residual 0.45%, so the saddle-fixed cos(theta_em) = -1 statement is robust to instanton-gas corrections at the tree-plus-one-loop precision of this paper.

CPT_robustness_bound.source: Standard CPT-violation bounds from electroweak precision tests; consistent with theta_em = pi at the experimentally accessible scale.

framing: The T-parity source axiom is the only physical input (beyond the five carrier coefficients and the bounce action S_bounce) that the HBR closure depends on; falsifying it (alternative T-parity assignment of Xi) would flip the sign of the two-loop closure and is the operational F2 falsification path of Section: Falsification.
